1. **Function $ L(t) $ for LED Light Output:**

The total illumination in the living room is the sum of the natural light $ N(t) $ and the LED light $ L(t) $. We want the total illumination to be constant at 500 lumens:

$$
N(t) + L(t) = 500
$$

Substitute the given function for $ N(t) $:

$$
300 + 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right) + L(t) = 500
$$

Solving for $ L(t) $:

$$
L(t) = 500 - 300 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right) = 200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)
$$

This function ensures that the LED light output $ L(t) $ adjusts dynamically to maintain a constant illumination of 500 lumens throughout the day.

**Total Energy Consumption Over 24 Hours:**

The LEDs consume power at a rate of 0.1 watts per lumen-hour. The total energy consumption is:

$$
\text{Total Energy} = \int_0^{24} L(t) \cdot 0.1 \, dt
$$

Substitute $ L(t) $:

$$
\text{Total Energy} = \int_0^{24} \left(200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)\right) \cdot 0.1 \, dt
$$

$$
= 0.1 \int_0^{24} \left(200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)\right) \, dt
$$

$$
= 0.1 \left[ 200t + 200 \int_0^{24} \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right) dt \right]
$$
